claims.evidence_text: The mean grain number per spike was 39.7 under control and 26.5 under water stress during meiosis.
claims.claim_summary: Mean wheat grain number per spike was 39.7 under normal watering.

claims.evidence_text: The mean grain number per spike was 39.7 under control and 26.5 under water stress during meiosis.
claims.claim_summary: Mean wheat grain number per spike fell to 26.5 under water stress during meiosis.

claims.evidence_text: Water stress is a yield-limiting factor and the most intense effects on yield have been recorded when stress coincides with the period between the onset of meiosis and early grain initiation
claims.claim_summary: Wheat yield is most sensitive to water stress during meiosis (reproductive stage) and early grain initiation.

claims.evidence_text: Plants were grown in polyvinyl chloride pots... in a controlled temperature glasshouse where day and night temperatures were maintained at 22±2˚C and 15±2˚C, respectively
claims.claim_summary: Wheat was grown at glasshouse day/night temperatures of 22°C / 15°C.

claims.evidence_text: Each pot... was filled with 2.1 kg of sterilised soil mixture (5: 2: 3 fine composted pine barks: coco peat: brown river sand, pH ~ 6.0).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat was grown in a soil mixture with pH approximately 6.0.

claims.evidence_text: The per cent water content of the soil mixture at field capacity was calculated as 26% (w/w)
claims.claim_summary: The soil mixture used for wheat had a field-capacity water content of 26% (w/w).

claims.evidence_text: By the next watering the minimum field capacity of the pots was around 60%. Based on the amount of moisture lost, each pot was watered to bring it up to 80% field capacity.
claims.claim_summary: Well-watered wheat pots were allowed to dry to ~60% field capacity then re-watered to 80% field capacity.

claims.evidence_text: water stress reduced wheat yield by 1.1 t/ha during the 2006/2007 cropping season in Australia, resulting in 46% yield reduction when compared to the previous (2005/2007 cropping season) year record
claims.claim_summary: Water stress reduced wheat yield by 1.1 t/ha (46%) in Australia in the 2006/2007 season.

claims.evidence_text: Vernalisation, the initiation of flowering by prolonged exposure to cold temperatures, is a major determinant of flowering time.
claims.claim_summary: Vernalization (prolonged cold exposure) is a major determinant of wheat flowering time; the Vrn-A1 gene lies on chromosome 5AL.
